dataList.desc: - A Singularity of a Wing capable of permanently erasing or destroying technologies. - Its potency and scope of effect depend on the price paid, but a reasonably powerful Concept Incinerator can erase every document related to the incinerated technology, photographs, and even memories—leaving behind nothing or, if something somehow does survive, a smudge and nothing more. Does the Concept Incinerator delete a concept like it never even existed, or does it just "smudge" our perception of it? - Faust elaborated with an example—deleting the general concept of, say, a "clock," requires literally everything even tangentially related to clocks getting chucked into the concept incinerator, but deleting only a specific model of a clock is as easy as tossing its blueprint in. In other words, a smaller scope equals easier incineration equals cheaper erasure.

- The Thumb, one of the five Fingers, is utterly obsessed with the concept of propriety. I've seen them popping people's arms out and blasting their jaws off just for questioning a superior. Though I've only met The Thumb members of the eastern branch, the Sinners tell me that they generally play by similar rules regardless of where they're based. - "Rank," or "Class," is something they're all really touchy about, which probably explains all those ridiculously detailed rules on the definitions, circumstances, types, and so on and so forth surrounding them. - According to those Soldato... Secando? Terzo? Anyways, according to those Thumb Soldatos we ran into in Daguanyuan, we apparently deserve to lose our jaws simply for invoking a Capo Quarto's—Lei Heng's—name without deference. - Hmm, I am the Manager of LCB... I wonder where I rank in their scale? Darn, I should've asked them when I had the chance...

- The Pinky is one of the five Fingers that follows the Virtue of Humanity (仁), which I'm still a bit unsure as to what it means. The point is, they're a bit more cryptic than the other Fingers when it comes to what they pursue.
- Instead of going around in their uniforms and advertising their true alignments like the other Fingers, they lurk anonymously in other organizations.
- There seems to be some condition and means of summoning the Pinkies all together, but when they're not summoned, they just lead their own lives as though they aren't even a part of this Finger. I don't know, can we even call them a real organized crime Syndicate?

- A bug that, when inserted into the human body, will share with its host the memories of the person who became its core.
- Immediately after insertion via the ear, the Anamnaworm will rapidly assimilate into the host, granting them all the memories and knowledge of the source individual used to make it. Convenient, but each Anamnaworm comes with the caveat that it cannot be removed by any other means than the conditions prescribed at its creation.
- Traditionally, all H Corp. Hierarchs were supposed to accept the "Xianhuang Worm," an Anamnaworm created from the founding Hierarch, into their bodies, but we managed to make it to the Tiekan Temple in time to prevent it from assimilating into Jia Xichun.
- In the chaotic aftermath that followed, the New League of Nine Littérateurs snatched the Xianhuang Worm away. Wei told me that, though the knowledge that once resided within the worm may be significant, the sheer time that has passed since its extraction from the previous host's body must have eroded much of it.

- There seem to be these mysterious figures known only as Heishou-Xianren, each managing a Pack and the corresponding Heishou Bolus. I wonder how they're distinguished from the Xianren of the Tiekan Temple?
- The right to "own" the Heishou Packs is called a "leash," and I think it's a kind of commodity that can be traded between people. Didn't get the chance to see for myself what those "leashes" look like, though.
- There seems to be a way to "un-leash" a Heishou and free them from service. But from what information I could gather from the Heishou Pack Identities, such generosity rarely ever happens because the price of freeing even a single Heishou is far too costly.

- They're "The Head." Located in... Well, I'd assume they're located in District 1.
- If anyone breaks a Taboo of the City—whenever, wherever—The Head will swoop in to punish the perpetrator.
- Normally, such infractions invite only the Claws, which are like enforcers from C Corp., but a Taboo violation of a sufficient scale might even end up summoning these beings called "Arbiters."
- I saw them in Hong Lu's memories of the Kong Family massacre. The moment the Kong Family "violated" a Taboo, the Head swooped in and destroyed all evidence that the transgression had ever occurred.

- E.G.O Gears extracted by the LCE or N Corp. both have traits that distinguish them from Lobotomy Corporation's E.G.O.
- LCE E.G.O gears use "Attunement Boost" to surge the source Abnormality's characteristics to the forefront, and the N Corp. E.G.O gear used by Jia Huan had an extra feature that generated a shield wall of sorts to protect its user in a moment of emergency.

- Jia Xichun ultimately won the Family Hierarch War and became H Corp.'s Hierarch, but had a slight stumble at the beginning of her reign due to Jia Yuanchun's machinations.
- Thankfully, as Jia Xichun managed to win the faith of the Four Families and the board of directors, the motion of no confidence Jia Yuanchun put forth failed to garner enough votes. Jia Xichun said she wishes to make Hongyuan a place where no one needs to live in fear, a place where one does not need to project fear... Perhaps H Corp. can finally leave behind its terrible past and change for the better?
- ... Speaking of which, are we ever going to leave this place? They've already held two separate farewell banquets for us, and after a few more excursions, I might need a third hand to count the number of times we'd bid our final good-byes to those guards at the gate. We're... we're not settling down here or anything, are we?
